docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
linax is a collection of state space models implemented in JAX. It is

  • is easy to use
  • ⚡ lightning fast
  • highly accessible.

Our aim as core developers is to provide researchers and scientists with a tool that just works out of the box, while simultaneously allowing for a high modularity and accessibility for anybody who wants to dig deeper.

docs.title: Just get me Going
docs.text: 🥱 If you don't care about the details, we provide example notebooks that are ready to use.

docs.title: Join the Community
docs.text: To join our growing community of JAX and state space model enthusiasts, feel free to join our server. Feel free to just write us a message (either there or to our personal email, see the bottom of this page) if you have any questions, comments, or want to say hi! 👋

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
linax is available as a PyPi package. To install it via uv, just run

uv add linax

or

uv add linax[cuda]

If pip is your package manager of choice, run

pip install linax

or

pip install linax[cuda]

docs.title: Full Library Installation
docs.text:
If you want to install the full libary, especially if you want to contribute to the project, clone the linax repository and cd into it

cd linax

If you want to install dependencies for CPU, run

uv sync

for GPU run

uv sync --extra cu12

To include development tooling (pre-commit, Ruff), install with the --dev flag:

uv sync --extra cu12 --dev

or

uv sync --dev

After installing the development dependencies (activate your environment if needed), enable the git hooks:

pre-commit install

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: If you want to contribute to the project, please check out contributing

docs.title: Core Contributors
docs.text:
This repository has been created and is maintained by:

  • Benedict Armstrong

This work has been carried out within the Computational Applied Mathematics & AI Lab, led by T. Konstantin Rusch.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: If you want to contribute to linax, clone the github directory and install the development dependencies as well as the pre-commit hooks (see getting started). We want to keep the project in a state that allows future users and collaborators to handle it easily.
